title: The Train Window
text_zh: 我坐了一趟很长的火车，从北方到南方。窗外的风景一直在变：先是灰色的城市，然后是黄色的田，再然后是绿色的山。车厢里大部分人在看手机或者睡觉，只有我一个人一直看着窗外。对面坐着一个老奶奶，她看了我半天，忽然说：「年轻人，你是第一个跟我一样喜欢看窗外的。」我笑了。她说：「我每年坐这趟火车去看女儿。每次窗外的景色都不太一样——去年那里还是一片田，今年变成了一栋楼。」她一边说一边看着窗外，好像在跟一个老朋友聊天。火车越走越远，窗外越来越绿，我觉得这比到目的地更有意思。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zuò le yí tàng hěn cháng de huǒchē, cóng běifāng dào nánfāng. Chuāng wài de fēngjǐng yìzhí zài biàn: xiān shì huīsè de chéngshì, ránhòu shì huángsè de tián, zài ránhòu shì lǜsè de shān. Chēxiāng lǐ dàbùfen rén zài kàn shǒujī huòzhě shuì jiào, zhǐyǒu wǒ yí gè rén yìzhí kànzhe chuāng wài. Duìmiàn zuòzhe yí gè lǎo nǎinai, tā kàn le wǒ bàntiān, hūrán shuō:「Niánqīng rén, nǐ shì dì-yī gè gēn wǒ yíyàng xǐhuan kàn chuāng wài de.」Wǒ xiào le. Tā shuō:「Wǒ měi nián zuò zhè tàng huǒchē qù kàn nǚ'ér. Měi cì chuāng wài de jǐngsè dōu bú tài yíyàng——qùnián nàlǐ háishi yí piàn tián, jīnnián biàn chéng le yí dòng lóu.」Tā yìbiān shuō yìbiān kànzhe chuāng wài, hǎoxiàng zài gēn yí gè lǎo péngyou liáotiān. Huǒchē yuè zǒu yuè yuǎn, chuāng wài yuè lái yuè lǜ, wǒ juéde zhè bǐ dào mùdìdì gèng yǒuyìsi.
text_en: I took a long train ride from north to south. The scenery outside kept changing: first grey cities, then yellow fields, then green mountains. Most people in the car were on phones or sleeping — only I kept watching the window. An old grandmother across from me watched me for a while, then said: 'Young man, you're the first person who likes watching outside as much as I do.' I smiled. She said: 'I take this train every year to see my daughter. The scenery is always a little different — last year that spot was a field, this year it's a building.' She talked while gazing out, as if chatting with an old friend. The train went farther and farther, the view greener and greener. I felt this was more interesting than arriving at the destination.

questions.q_zh: 车厢里大部分人在做什么？
questions.q_en: What were most people in the car doing?

questions.q_zh: 老奶奶每年坐火车去做什么？
questions.q_en: Why does the grandmother take the train each year?

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得什么比到目的地更有意思？
questions.q_en: What does 'I' find more interesting than arriving?

options.text: 看窗外风景变化的过程
options.pinyin: kàn chuāng wài fēngjǐng biànhuà de guòchéng

title: The Alley Barber
text_zh: 老城区的巷子里有一家理发店，只有一把椅子、一面镜子和一位老师傅。剪一次头发只要十五块钱，比外面便宜得多。但是来这里的人不只是因为便宜。老师傅剪头发的时候不说话，手很稳，剪完以后会用热毛巾帮你擦脸。整个过程大概二十分钟，很安静。有一个年轻人从小就在这里剪头发，现在已经三十多岁了。他说：「外面那些理发店又贵又吵，还总是问我要不要做什么项目。这里什么都不问，坐下来就剪。」老师傅听到了，笑着说：「你头发什么样我比你清楚，不需要问。」那家店没有牌子，但是认识它的人越来越多。
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū de xiàngzi lǐ yǒu yì jiā lǐfà diàn, zhǐyǒu yì bǎ yǐzi, yí miàn jìngzi hé yí wèi lǎo shīfu. Jiǎn yí cì tóufa zhǐ yào shíwǔ kuài qián, bǐ wàimiàn piányi de duō. Dànshì lái zhèlǐ de rén bù zhǐshì yīnwèi piányi. Lǎo shīfu jiǎn tóufa de shíhou bù shuō huà, shǒu hěn wěn, jiǎn wán yǐhòu huì yòng rè máojīn bāng nǐ cā liǎn. Zhěng gè guòchéng dàgài èrshí fēnzhōng, hěn ānjìng. Yǒu yí gè niánqīng rén cóng xiǎo jiù zài zhèlǐ jiǎn tóufa, xiànzài yǐjīng sānshí duō suì le. Tā shuō:「Wàimiàn nàxiē lǐfà diàn yòu guì yòu chǎo, hái zǒng shì wèn wǒ yào bu yào zuò shénme xiàngmù. Zhèlǐ shénme dōu bú wèn, zuò xiàlái jiù jiǎn.」Lǎo shīfu tīng dào le, xiàozhe shuō:「Nǐ tóufa shénme yàng wǒ bǐ nǐ qīngchu, bù xūyào wèn.」Nà jiā diàn méiyǒu páizi, dànshì rènshi tā de rén yuè lái yuè duō.
text_en: In an old district alley there's a barbershop with just one chair, one mirror, and one old barber. A haircut costs only fifteen yuan — much cheaper than elsewhere. But people don't come just for the price. The old barber doesn't talk while cutting, his hands are steady, and afterward he wipes your face with a hot towel. The whole process takes about twenty minutes, very quiet. One young man has been getting haircuts here since childhood — he's now in his thirties. He says: 'Outside shops are expensive and noisy, always asking if I want some extra treatment. Here they ask nothing — sit down and cut.' The barber overheard and smiled: 'I know your hair better than you do — no need to ask.' The shop has no sign, but more and more people know about it.

questions.q_en: How much is a haircut?

questions.q_zh: 老师傅剪完头发以后做什么？
questions.q_en: What does the barber do after cutting?

options.pinyin: yòng rè máojīn bāng nǐ cā liǎn
options.text_en: wipes your face with a hot towel

questions.q_zh: 年轻人为什么喜欢这家店？
questions.q_en: Why does the young man like this shop?

options.text: 因为什么都不问，坐下来就剪
options.pinyin: yīnwèi shénme dōu bú wèn, zuò xiàlái jiù jiǎn
options.text_en: asks nothing, just sit and cut

title: The Wrong Delivery
text_zh: 今天中午我点了一份炒饭。外卖送到的时候，我打开一看——是一碗面条。我打电话给外卖员，他说：「不好意思，可能跟别人的换了。」我说没关系。但是五分钟以后，我接到一个陌生人的电话：「你好，我点的是面条，但是收到了炒饭。你是不是收到了我的面条？」我说是的。她笑了：「那我们换回来？」我们约在楼下见面。到了以后我发现，她就住在我隔壁。我们认识了十秒钟就把外卖换了回来。她说：「我搬来一年了，今天第一次跟邻居说话。」我说：「如果不是送错了外卖，我们可能永远不会认识。」
text_pinyin: Jīntiān zhōngwǔ wǒ diǎn le yí fèn chǎo fàn. Wàimài sòng dào de shíhou, wǒ dǎkāi yí kàn——shì yì wǎn miàntiáo. Wǒ dǎ diànhuà gěi wàimàiyuán, tā shuō:「Bù hǎoyìsi, kěnéng gēn biéren de huàn le.」Wǒ shuō méi guānxi. Dànshì wǔ fēnzhōng yǐhòu, wǒ jiē dào yí gè mòshēng rén de diànhuà:「Nǐ hǎo, wǒ diǎn de shì miàntiáo, dànshì shōu dào le chǎo fàn. Nǐ shì bú shì shōu dào le wǒ de miàntiáo?」Wǒ shuō shì de. Tā xiào le:「Nà wǒmen huàn huílái?」Wǒmen yuē zài lóu xià jiànmiàn. Dào le yǐhòu wǒ fāxiàn, tā jiù zhù zài wǒ gébì. Wǒmen rènshi le shí miǎozhōng jiù bǎ wàimài huàn le huílái. Tā shuō:「Wǒ bān lái yì nián le, jīntiān dì-yī cì gēn línjū shuō huà.」Wǒ shuō:「Rúguǒ búshì sòng cuò le wàimài, wǒmen kěnéng yǒngyuǎn bú huì rènshi.」
text_en: Today at noon I ordered fried rice. When the delivery arrived, I opened it — it was a bowl of noodles. I called the delivery person. He said: 'Sorry, it probably got switched with someone else's.' I said no problem. But five minutes later, a stranger called: 'Hi, I ordered noodles but got fried rice. Did you get my noodles?' I said yes. She laughed: 'Swap back?' We agreed to meet downstairs. When I got there, I found she lives right next door. We met and swapped in ten seconds. She said: 'I've lived here a year and this is the first time I've talked to a neighbor.' I said: 'If the delivery hadn't been mixed up, we might never have met.'

questions.q_en: What did 'I' receive?
Where does the stranger live?

questions.q_zh: 她搬来多久第一次跟邻居说话？
questions.q_en: How long had she lived there before talking to a neighbor?

title: The Kindergarten Crossing Guard
title_zh: 幼儿园门口的交通志愿者
text_zh: 每天早上八点，幼儿园门口都有一个穿黄色马甲的老人在帮小朋友过马路。他不是交警，也不是学校的人——他是住在附近的退休老师。他说：「这条路车比较多，小朋友个子矮，司机不容易看到。」每次有小朋友过马路，他都会举起手里的小旗子，让车停下来。小朋友们都叫他「旗子爷爷」。有一天下大雨，他还是来了，全身都被淋湿了。幼儿园的园长给他送了一把伞，说：「下这么大的雨，您可以不来的。」他说：「越是下雨，路上越危险。除了我以外，还有谁来？」那把伞比较小，但是他把它举在了小朋友头上。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang bā diǎn, yòu'éryuán ménkǒu dōu yǒu yí gè chuān huángsè mǎjiǎ de lǎorén zài bāng xiǎopéngyou guò mǎlù. Tā búshì jiāojǐng, yě búshì xuéxiào de rén——tā shì zhù zài fùjìn de tuìxiū lǎoshī. Tā shuō:「Zhè tiáo lù chē bǐjiào duō, xiǎopéngyou gèzi ǎi, sījī bù róngyì kàn dào.」Měi cì yǒu xiǎopéngyou guò mǎlù, tā dōu huì jǔ qǐ shǒu lǐ de xiǎo qízi, ràng chē tíng xiàlái. Xiǎopéngyoumen dōu jiào tā「qízi yéye」. Yǒu yì tiān xià dà yǔ, tā háishi lái le, quán shēn dōu bèi lín shī le. Yòu'éryuán de yuánzhǎng gěi tā sòng le yì bǎ sǎn, shuō:「Xià zhème dà de yǔ, nín kěyǐ bù lái de.」Tā shuō:「Yuè shì xià yǔ, lù shàng yuè wēixiǎn. Chúle wǒ yǐwài, hái yǒu shéi lái?」Nà bǎ sǎn bǐjiào xiǎo, dànshì tā bǎ tā jǔ zài le xiǎopéngyou tóu shàng.
text_en: Every morning at eight, an old man in a yellow vest helps children cross the road in front of the kindergarten. He's not a traffic officer or school staff — he's a retired teacher from the neighborhood. He says: 'This road has a lot of cars, and kids are short — drivers can't easily see them.' Whenever children cross, he raises his little flag to stop traffic. The kids all call him 'Flag Grandpa.' One day it rained hard. He still came, drenched from head to toe. The kindergarten principal brought him an umbrella: 'It's raining so hard, you don't have to come.' He said: 'The harder it rains, the more dangerous the road. Besides me, who else would come?' The umbrella was rather small, but he held it over the children's heads.

questions.q_zh: 那个老人以前是做什么的？
questions.q_en: What did the old man used to do?
What do the children call him?

questions.q_zh: 下大雨的时候他怎么做？
questions.q_en: What did he do in the heavy rain?

options.pinyin: bǎ sǎn jǔ zài zìjǐ tóu shàng

options.text: 还是来了，把伞举在小朋友头上
options.pinyin: háishi lái le, bǎ sǎn jǔ zài xiǎopéngyou tóu shàng
options.text_en: still came, held umbrella over children

title: The Night Noodle Stall
text_zh: 凌晨一点，城市大部分地方都关了。但是有一个面摊还亮着灯。老板是一个五十多岁的男人，他只卖一种面——阳春面。就是最简单的那种，面条、葱花、酱油、一点猪油。来吃的人都是深夜下班的人：出租车司机、医院的护士、加班的白领。大家坐在塑料椅子上，谁也不说话，安安静静地吃完就走。但是每个人走的时候，都会对老板说一声「谢谢」或者「明天见」。有人问老板：「你怎么不多卖几种面？」他说：「夜里来的人不需要选择，他们需要的是一碗热的、比较简单的东西。」我觉得他说得比较对。有时候，越简单的东西越温暖。
text_pinyin: Língchén yī diǎn, chéngshì dàbùfen dìfang dōu guān le. Dànshì yǒu yí gè miàn tān hái liàngzhe dēng. Lǎobǎn shì yí gè wǔshí duō suì de nánrén, tā zhǐ mài yì zhǒng miàn——yángchūn miàn. Jiùshì zuì jiǎndān de nà zhǒng, miàntiáo, cōnghuā, jiàngyóu, yìdiǎn zhūyóu. Lái chī de rén dōu shì shēnyè xiàbān de rén: chūzū chē sījī, yīyuàn de hùshi, jiābān de báilǐng. Dàjiā zuò zài sùliào yǐzi shàng, shéi yě bù shuō huà, ān'ānjìngjìng de chī wán jiù zǒu. Dànshì měi gè rén zǒu de shíhou, dōu huì duì lǎobǎn shuō yì shēng「xièxie」huòzhě「míngtiān jiàn」. Yǒu rén wèn lǎobǎn:「Nǐ zěnme bù duō mài jǐ zhǒng miàn?」Tā shuō:「Yè lǐ lái de rén bù xūyào xuǎnzé, tāmen xūyào de shì yì wǎn rè de, bǐjiào jiǎndān de dōngxi.」Wǒ juéde tā shuō de bǐjiào duì. Yǒu shíhou, yuè jiǎndān de dōngxi yuè wēnnuǎn.
text_en: At one in the morning, most of the city is closed. But one noodle stall still has its light on. The owner is a man in his fifties who sells only one kind of noodle — plain noodle soup. The simplest kind: noodles, scallion, soy sauce, a bit of lard. The customers are all late-night workers: taxi drivers, hospital nurses, office workers pulling overtime. Everyone sits on plastic chairs, nobody talks, quietly finishes and leaves. But every person says 'thank you' or 'see you tomorrow' to the owner on the way out. Someone asked: 'Why not sell more kinds of noodles?' He said: 'People who come at night don't need choices. They need a bowl of something hot and rather simple.' I think he's rather right. Sometimes the simpler something is, the warmer it feels.

questions.q_en: How many kinds of noodles does the owner sell?
Who are the customers?

questions.q_zh: 老板为什么只卖一种面？
questions.q_en: Why does the owner sell only one kind?

options.pinyin: tā zhǐ huì zuò yì zhǒng
options.text_en: he can only make one kind

options.text: 夜里来的人不需要选择，需要简单的东西
options.pinyin: yè lǐ lái de rén bù xūyào xuǎnzé, xūyào jiǎndān de dōngxi
options.text_en: night customers don't need choices, need simple things

options.pinyin: zhè zhǒng miàn zuì zhuàn qián

title: The Old Jacket
text_zh: 我有一件穿了八年的旧外套。它已经比较旧了——颜色变淡了，袖子上有一个小洞，拉链有时候拉不上去。朋友们都让我扔了买新的。但是我不想。这件外套是我大学毕业那年买的，那时候我刚找到第一份工作，用第一个月的工资买的。我穿着它去面试过、搬过家、在冬天的街上走过很远的路。它不好看了，但是它陪我经历了很多事情。有人说衣服只是衣服，但是对我来说，这件外套就像一个朋友。我把袖子上的洞补好了，把拉链修好了。它还能再穿几年。有些东西旧了不是应该扔掉，而是应该修好。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu yí jiàn chuān le bā nián de jiù wàitào. Tā yǐjīng bǐjiào jiù le——yánsè biàn dàn le, xiùzi shàng yǒu yí gè xiǎo dòng, lāliàn yǒu shíhou lā bú shàngqù. Péngyoumen dōu ràng wǒ rēng le mǎi xīn de. Dànshì wǒ bù xiǎng. Zhè jiàn wàitào shì wǒ dàxué bìyè nà nián mǎi de, nà shíhou wǒ gāng zhǎo dào dì-yī fèn gōngzuò, yòng dì-yī gè yuè de gōngzī mǎi de. Wǒ chuānzhe tā qù miànshì guò, bān guò jiā, zài dōngtiān de jiē shàng zǒu guò hěn yuǎn de lù. Tā bù hǎokàn le, dànshì tā péi wǒ jīnglì le hěn duō shìqing. Yǒu rén shuō yīfu zhǐshì yīfu, dànshì duì wǒ lái shuō, zhè jiàn wàitào jiù xiàng yí gè péngyou. Wǒ bǎ xiùzi shàng de dòng bǔ hǎo le, bǎ lāliàn xiū hǎo le. Tā hái néng zài chuān jǐ nián. Yǒuxiē dōngxi jiù le búshì yīnggāi rēng diào, ér shì yīnggāi xiū hǎo.
text_en: I have a jacket I've worn for eight years. It's rather old now — the color has faded, there's a small hole in the sleeve, and the zipper sometimes sticks. Friends all tell me to throw it out and buy new. But I don't want to. I bought this jacket the year I graduated college, when I'd just found my first job — paid for with my first month's salary. I've worn it to interviews, moved houses in it, walked long winter streets in it. It doesn't look good anymore, but it's been through a lot with me. Some say clothes are just clothes, but to me this jacket is like a friend. I patched the hole in the sleeve, fixed the zipper. It can last a few more years. Some things, when they get old, shouldn't be thrown away — they should be repaired.

questions.q_zh: 这件外套是用什么买的？
questions.q_en: What was the jacket bought with?
What's wrong with the jacket?

options.text: 颜色变淡了，袖子有洞，拉链不好用
options.pinyin: yánsè biàn dàn le, xiùzi yǒu dòng, lāliàn bù hǎo yòng
options.text_en: color faded, hole in sleeve, zipper sticks

questions.q_en: What did 'I' do in the end?

options.text_en: threw it out and bought new

options.text: 把洞补好了，拉链修好了
options.pinyin: bǎ dòng bǔ hǎo le, lāliàn xiū hǎo le
options.text_en: patched the hole, fixed the zipper

options.pinyin: fàng zài yīguì lǐ bù chuān le

title: The Elevator Smile
text_zh: 我每天早上坐电梯下楼。电梯里的人都低着头看手机，谁也不看谁。有一天，一个新搬来的女生进了电梯，对每个人微笑着说了一声「早上好」。大家都愣了一下——在电梯里跟陌生人说话，这在城市里比较少见。有人笑了笑，有人点了点头，有人还是没有反应。但是第二天，她又说了。第三天也是。慢慢地，电梯里的气氛变了。有人开始也说「早上好」了。一个月以后，我发现自己也开始说了。一个人的习惯改变了一部电梯的气氛。有时候让世界变好的方法比较简单——你先开口就行了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān zǎoshang zuò diàntī xià lóu. Diàntī lǐ de rén dōu dīzhe tóu kàn shǒujī, shéi yě bú kàn shéi. Yǒu yì tiān, yí gè xīn bān lái de nǚshēng jìn le diàntī, duì měi gè rén wēixiàozhe shuō le yì shēng「zǎoshang hǎo」. Dàjiā dōu lèng le yíxià——zài diàntī lǐ gēn mòshēng rén shuō huà, zhè zài chéngshì lǐ bǐjiào shǎojiàn. Yǒu rén xiào le xiào, yǒu rén diǎn le diǎn tóu, yǒu rén háishi méiyǒu fǎnyìng. Dànshì dì-èr tiān, tā yòu shuō le. Dì-sān tiān yěshì. Mànman de, diàntī lǐ de qìfēn biàn le. Yǒu rén kāishǐ yě shuō「zǎoshang hǎo」le. Yí gè yuè yǐhòu, wǒ fāxiàn zìjǐ yě kāishǐ shuō le. Yí gè rén de xíguàn gǎibiàn le yí bù diàntī de qìfēn. Yǒu shíhou ràng shìjiè biàn hǎo de fāngfǎ bǐjiào jiǎndān——nǐ xiān kāi kǒu jiù xíng le.
text_en: Every morning I take the elevator down. Everyone looks down at their phones, nobody looks at anyone. One day, a girl who just moved in entered and smiled at everyone saying 'good morning.' People were taken aback — talking to strangers in elevators is rather uncommon in cities. Some smiled back, some nodded, some didn't react. But the next day, she said it again. The third day too. Gradually, the atmosphere in the elevator changed. People started saying 'good morning' too. After a month, I found myself saying it as well. One person's habit changed an entire elevator's atmosphere. Sometimes the way to make the world better is rather simple — you just speak first.

questions.q_zh: 电梯里的人以前做什么？
questions.q_en: What did people in the elevator used to do?

options.text_en: said good morning to each other

questions.q_en: What did the girl do?

options.pinyin: měi tiān zài diàntī lǐ chàng gē
měi tiān wēixiàozhe shuō zǎoshang hǎo
options.text_en: smiled and said good morning daily

options.pinyin: zài diàntī lǐ tiē le zhǐtiáo

questions.q_zh: 一个月以后电梯里怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What was the elevator like after a month?

options.text: 大家都开始说早上好了
options.pinyin: dàjiā dōu kāishǐ shuō zǎoshang hǎo le

title: The Umbrella Stand
text_zh: 我们小区的大门旁边出现了一个伞架。上面贴着一张纸：「下雨的时候拿，不下雨的时候还。」没有人知道是谁放的。刚开始只有三把伞，都是旧的。有一天下雨了，伞被人拿走了两把。第二天没下雨，那两把伞被放回来了。而且多了一把新的——有人觉得旧伞不太好用，就放了一把自己的新伞。一个星期以后，伞架上已经有十几把伞了。各种颜色、各种大小。有的人还在自己的伞上写了名字。物业的人说：「我们没有安排这个事情，但是我们觉得应该留着。」那个伞架比较旧了，但是上面的伞越来越多。这大概就是信任吧——你先给出去，然后别人也会给回来。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū de dàmén pángbiān chūxiàn le yí gè sǎn jià. Shàngmiàn tiēzhe yì zhāng zhǐ:「Xià yǔ de shíhou ná, bú xià yǔ de shíhou huán.」Méiyǒu rén zhīdào shì shéi fàng de. Gāng kāishǐ zhǐyǒu sān bǎ sǎn, dōu shì jiù de. Yǒu yì tiān xià yǔ le, sǎn bèi rén ná zǒu le liǎng bǎ. Dì-èr tiān méi xià yǔ, nà liǎng bǎ sǎn bèi fàng huílái le. Érqiě duō le yì bǎ xīn de——yǒu rén juéde jiù sǎn bú tài hǎo yòng, jiù fàng le yì bǎ zìjǐ de xīn sǎn. Yí gè xīngqī yǐhòu, sǎn jià shàng yǐjīng yǒu shí jǐ bǎ sǎn le. Gè zhǒng yánsè, gè zhǒng dàxiǎo. Yǒude rén hái zài zìjǐ de sǎn shàng xiě le míngzi. Wùyè de rén shuō:「Wǒmen méiyǒu ānpái zhège shìqing, dànshì wǒmen juéde yīnggāi liúzhe.」Nàge sǎn jià bǐjiào jiù le, dànshì shàngmiàn de sǎn yuè lái yuè duō. Zhè dàgài jiùshì xìnrèn ba——nǐ xiān gěi chūqù, ránhòu biéren yě huì gěi huílái.
text_en: An umbrella stand appeared by our complex's main gate. A note on it reads: 'Take when it rains, return when it doesn't.' Nobody knows who put it there. At first there were only three umbrellas, all old. One rainy day, two were taken. The next day, when it didn't rain, both came back. Plus one new one — someone thought the old ones weren't great, so they added their own new umbrella. After a week, there were already a dozen umbrellas on the stand. All colors, all sizes. Some people even wrote their names on theirs. The property manager said: 'We didn't arrange this, but we think it should stay.' That stand is rather old, but the umbrellas keep growing. This is probably what trust is — you give first, and others give back.

questions.q_en: What does the note on the stand say?

options.text: 下雨的时候拿，不下雨的时候还

title: The Clockmaker
text_zh: 老城区有一个修钟表的老人，今年八十二岁了。他的店只有一张桌子大，桌子上放满了各种钟表的零件。他每天早上九点开门，下午五点关门，中间不休息。他的手比较抖了，但是拿起工具的时候又变得很稳。有人拿了一个不走了的旧钟来找他，说：「这个钟比较旧了，还能修吗？」他把钟打开看了看，说：「只是一个小零件坏了。它还想走，只是需要有人帮它一下。」他花了一个下午把那个钟修好了。走的时候，那个人说：「这个钟是我爷爷的。谢谢您让它又走了起来。」老人笑着说：「不是我厉害，是那个钟不想停。」
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū yǒu yí gè xiū zhōngbiǎo de lǎorén, jīnnián bāshí'èr suì le. Tā de diàn zhǐyǒu yì zhāng zhuōzi dà, zhuōzi shàng fàng mǎn le gè zhǒng zhōngbiǎo de língjiàn. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang jiǔ diǎn kāi mén, xiàwǔ wǔ diǎn guān mén, zhōngjiān bù xiūxi. Tā de shǒu bǐjiào dǒu le, dànshì ná qǐ gōngjù de shíhou yòu biàn de hěn wěn. Yǒu rén ná le yí gè bù zǒu le de jiù zhōng lái zhǎo tā, shuō:「Zhège zhōng bǐjiào jiù le, hái néng xiū ma?」Tā bǎ zhōng dǎkāi kàn le kàn, shuō:「Zhǐshì yí gè xiǎo língjiàn huài le. Tā hái xiǎng zǒu, zhǐshì xūyào yǒu rén bāng tā yíxià.」Tā huā le yí gè xiàwǔ bǎ nàge zhōng xiū hǎo le. Zǒu de shíhou, nàge rén shuō:「Zhège zhōng shì wǒ yéye de. Xièxie nín ràng tā yòu zǒu le qǐlái.」Lǎorén xiàozhe shuō:「Búshì wǒ lìhai, shì nàge zhōng bù xiǎng tíng.」
text_en: In the old district there's a watchmaker, eighty-two this year. His shop is just one table big, covered with clock parts. He opens at nine, closes at five, no break in between. His hands shake rather noticeably now, but when he picks up tools they become steady again. Someone brought a stopped old clock and asked: 'It's rather old — can it still be fixed?' He opened it, looked, and said: 'Just a small part is broken. It still wants to run — it just needs someone to help.' He spent an afternoon fixing it. Leaving, the person said: 'This clock was my grandfather's. Thank you for making it run again.' The old man smiled: 'I'm not that skilled — it's the clock that doesn't want to stop.'

questions.q_en: What are the old man's hands like?

options.text: 平时抖，拿工具的时候变稳
options.pinyin: píngshí dǒu, ná gōngjù de shíhou biàn wěn

questions.q_en: What was wrong with the old clock?

options.pinyin: zhǐshì yí gè xiǎo língjiàn huài le
tài jiù le bù néng xiū

questions.q_zh: 老人说钟能修好是因为什么？
questions.q_en: Why did the old man say the clock could be fixed?

options.text_en: the clock doesn't want to stop
